document.docAuthor: De Moraes, Pedro L. R. & Trinca, Mayra D.
document.masterDocTitle: Foliar cuticles of species of Persea subg. Eriodaphne (Lauraceae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Foliar cuticles of species of Persea subg. Eriodaphne (Lauraceae)

heading.emphasis:
Key to the American species of
Persea
, based on foliar cuticle characters

paragraph.keyLead: – Overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to broadly circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type AF (almost flat) to BCP3 (subsidiary cells broadly circular, protruding but interrupted at both ends of the aperture field) to NCP4 (narrowly circular, protruding but interrupted at both ends of the aperture field) ............................................................................................................4

5. Epidermal non-specialised cells strictly of almost equal size on both surfaces, anticlinal walls straight to slightly curved on both surfaces..............................................................................................................................................................................
P. borbonia

– Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger adaxially, anticlinal walls straight to slightly curved to undulate adaxially...........................................................................................................................................................
P. longipes

6. Epidermal non-specialised cells strictly larger adaxially, anticlinal walls strictly straight adaxially ...................................
P. pumila

– Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger adaxially, anticlinal walls straight to slightly curved on both surfaces.....................................................................................................................................................................
P. venosa

7. Surface appearance of epidermal periclinal walls strictly papillose ..................................................................................
P. povedae
– Surface appearance of epidermal periclinal walls predominantly protruding upward.......................................................................8
– Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger adaxially .............................................................10

9. Anticlinal walls straight to slightly curved on both surfaces, stomatal surface type NCP4 ..................................................
P. glabra

– Anticlinal walls strictly straight adaxially, stomatal surface type AF , BCP3 or NCP4 .........................................................
P. pumila

11.== Anticlinal walls strictly straight adaxially, stomatal ledge lip-shaped ................................................................................
P. cuneata

–== Anticlinal walls straight to slightly curved on both surfaces, stomatal ledge lip-shaped to butterfly-shaped ......................
P. venosa
13. Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger abaxially .............................................................14
– Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger adaxially .............................................................15

– Anticlinal walls strictly straight adaxially, stomatal surface type AF , BCP3 , NCP4 or RH3 (rhombic, protruding, irregular margin), average stomatal index 0.20 ± 0.035 .....................................................................................................................................
P. aurata
15. Epidermal non-specialised cells strictly of almost equal size on both surfaces...............................................................................16
– Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger adaxially .............................................................26
– Surface of epidermal periclinal walls flat to protruding, or protruding to papillose........................................................................20

18. Anticlinal walls strictly straight adaxially, straight to slightly curved abaxially, stomatal ledge lip-, butterfly-, and/or bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to elliptic to rhombic, stomatal surface type BCP3 or NCP4 ....................... .............................................................................................................................................................................
P. pseudofasciculata

19. Overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type CP (circular and protruding forming a symmetric circle) or NCP4 .....................................................................................................................................................................
P. sericea

– Overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to rhombic, stomatal surface type BCP3 or NCP4 ....................
P. vesticula

– Surface of epidermal periclinal walls protruding to papillose...................................................................................
P. rufotomentosa

21. Stomatal ledge strictly lip-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type BCP3 or NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.17 ± 0.03.........................................................................................................................
P. fusca

– Stomatal ledge lip- to butterfly- or bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to broadly circular to elliptic to rhombic, stomatal surface type AF , BCP3 , NCP4 or RH3, average stomatal index different .........................................................22

22. Stomatal ledge strictly bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular, stomatal surface type AF or BCP3 , average stomatal index 0.18 ± 0.04 ................................................................................................................................
P. meridensis

23. Stomatal ledge lip- to butterfly-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type AF , BCP3 or NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.16 ± 0.02 ...............................................................................................................
P. fulva

– Stomatal ledge lip- to bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to broadly circular to elliptic to rhombic, average stomatal index of another value........................................................................................................................................................24

24. Stomatal surface type AF , NCP4 or RH3, average stomatal index 0.14 ± 0.0041 .......................................................
P. weberbaueri
– Epidermal non-specialised cells of almost equal size on both surfaces to larger adaxially .............................................................30
– Stomatal surface type NCP4 to RH3, average stomatal index of another value ..............................................................................29

29. Overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to elliptic, average stomatal index 0.16 ± 0.01.................................
P. perseiphylla

– Overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to rhombic, average stomatal index 0.08 ± 0.0015.........................
P. ruizii

30. Stomatal ledge lip- to butterfly-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to rhombic, stomatal surface type LP1 or NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.18 ± 0.03 ........................................................................................................
P. willdenovii
– Stomatal ledge strictly lip- or lip- to bat-shaped, average stomatal index different.........................................................................31

– Overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to broadly circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type AF , BCP3 or NCP4 , average stomatal index different ....................................................................................................................................................................32

35. Anticlinal walls curved to undulate adaxially, curved abaxially.........................................................................................
P. croizatii
– Anticlinal walls strictly straight or straight to slightly curved adaxially, straight to slightly curved abaxially (when visible) .......36

36. Stomatal ledge lip-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex elliptic...................................................................
P. microneura
– Stomatal ledge bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular.......................................................................37

– Stomatal surface type BCP3 , average stomatal index unknown (cells not counted) ..................................................
P. chrysophylla

39. Anticlinal walls straight adaxially, stomatal ledge bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular, stomatal surface type BCP3 or NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.17 ± 0.01.........................................................................................
P. albida

– Anticlinal walls curved to undulate adaxially, stomatal ledge lip-shaped to bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex elliptic, stomatal surface type AF or NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.20 ± 0.02............................................................
P. buchtienii
– Epidermal non-specialised cells equal size on both surfaces or almost equal to larger adaxially ...................................................45

42. Anticlinal walls straight to undulate adaxially, curved abaxially, average stomatal index 0.27 ± 0.02 .........................
P. schiedeana
– Anticlinal walls strictly straight or straight to curved on both surfaces, average stomatal index different .....................................43

43. Stomatal ledge butterfly-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.17 ± 0.02 ........................................................................................................................................
P. veraguasensis

– Stomatal ledge lip-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to rhombic, stomatal surface type BCP3 , NCP4 or RH3, average stomatal index same or different ...........................................................................................................................44
– Surface of epidermal periclinal walls flat to protruding or protruding to papillose.........................................................................49

46. Stomatal ledge bat-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to elliptic, stomatal surface type BCP3 or NCP4 , average stomatal index 0.13 ± 0.02 .................................................................................................................................
P. liebmannii

– Stomatal ledge strictly lip-shaped or lip-shaped to butterfly-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex strictly elliptic or circular to elliptic, stomatal surface appearance type NCP4 , average stomatal index different ......................................................47
– Stomatal ledge strictly lip-shaped, overall shape of the stomatal complex strictly elliptic, average stomatal index different........48

48. Anticlinal walls strictly straight adaxially, average stomatal index 0.21 ± 0.01 ..........................................................
P. oblongifolia

– Anticlinal walls straight to curved adaxially, average stomatal index 0.21 ± 0.03 ....................................................
P. psammophila

51. Anticlinal walls straight adaxially, average stomatal index 0.26 ± 0.03 .........................................................................
P. americana

– Anticlinal walls straight to curved on both surfaces, average stomatal index 0.20 ± 0.01 .................................................
P. julianae

53. Overall shape of the stomatal complex elliptic, average stomatal index 0.16 ± 0.03 ............................................................
P. caesia
– Overall shape of the stomatal complex broadly circular to elliptic, average stomatal index different ............................................54

54. Stomatal ledge butterfly-shaped to bat-shaped, average stomatal index 0.18 ± 0.03 ............................................................
P. rigida
– Stomatal ledge lip-shaped to bat-shaped, average stomatal index different.....................................................................................55
